title: Lithospermum calycosum
taxonomy: Lithospermum calycosum belongs to the family Boraginaceae. This plant is a species of flowering plant in the borage family. It is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The classification of Lithospermum calycosum includes the kingdom Plantae, and the order Boraginales. Within the order Boraginales, Lithospermum calycosum is placed in the family Boraginaceae.

intro: Lithospermum calycosum, also known as Gromwell, is a perennial herbaceous plant native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immune-modulating properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of arthritis, wounds, and infections. The bioactive constituents of Lithospermum calycosum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and glycosides, such as lithospermic acid, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Lithospermum calycosum is commonly prepared in herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as in topical creams and ointments, to support skin health and wound healing.
